name: Sanguine Gothic
rationale: Anchors the alignment beat in the visual language of the World of Darkness — Vampire: the Masquerade's gothic-punk personal horror — because that line owns the idea this beat needs: the monster is the self, the Beast lives behind the civilized mask, and restraint is a discipline renewed nightly, not a cure. Ink-heavy, blood-and-shadow, beautiful and damned; the aesthetic that makes 'the helpful smile with teeth behind it' read instantly.
preamble: Gothic-punk personal-horror illustration in the World of Darkness tradition — Vampire: the Masquerade 20th-anniversary house style, Tim Bradstreet ink realism meets John Blanche grit; heavy black ink chiaroscuro with a tight blood-red, bone-white, and candle-gold palette, everything else swallowed by shadow; ornate decadent detail — crimson velvet, black lace, baroque candelabra, cracked porcelain masks, gothic tracery — rendered with stark high-contrast hatching and selective desaturation; mood: seductive, damned, intimate dread; a beautiful face and the predator behind it in the same frame; references: VtM 20th Anniversary art, Tim Bradstreet covers, Werewolf: the Apocalypse rage-and-shadow plates, gothic graphic-novel inking; composition favors a single dramatic figure carved from darkness by one warm light source, the mask and the thing beneath it both legible at once.
